creator: tomek@cedro.info
text:
Hello Johannes, thanks for quick response, here goes the outputs :-)




##### drm and xf86-video-intel and xorg.conf removed.

# pkg info -x intel
intel-backlight-20180328
# pkg info -x drm
libdrm-2.4.98_1,1
i# ls /etc/X11/
xorg.conf_


cd@0xCFMX4:~ % startx
xauth:  file /home/cd/.serverauth.15443 does not exist

#### no xorg.conf, no video-intel, added drm-kmod.

# pkg install drm-kmod
Updating FreeBSD repository catalogue...
FreeBSD repository is up to date.
All repositories are up to date.
The following 2 package(s) will be affected (of 0 checked):

New packages to be INSTALLED:
        drm-kmod: g20190710
        drm-fbsd12.0-kmod: 4.16.g20190814

Number of packages to be installed: 2

The process will require 10 MiB more space.
812 B to be downloaded.

Proceed with this action? [y/N]: y
[1/1] Fetching drm-kmod-g20190710.txz: 100%    812 B   0.8kB/s    00:01
Checking integrity... done (0 conflicting)
[1/2] Installing drm-fbsd12.0-kmod-4.16.g20190814...
[1/2] Extracting drm-fbsd12.0-kmod-4.16.g20190814: 100%
[2/2] Installing drm-kmod-g20190710...
Message from drm-fbsd12.0-kmod-4.16.g20190814:

The drm-fbsd12.0-kmod port can be enabled for amdgpu (for AMD GPUs starting
with the HD7000 series / Tahiti) or i915kms (for Intel APUs starting with
HD3000 / Sandy Bridge) through kld_list in /etc/rc.conf. radeonkms for older
AMD GPUs can be loaded and there are some positive reports if EFI boot is NOT
enabled (similar to amdgpu).

For amdgpu: kld_list="amdgpu"
For Intel: kld_list="/boot/modules/i915kms.ko"
For radeonkms: kld_list="/boot/modules/radeonkms.ko"

Please ensure that all users requiring graphics are members of the
"video" group.

Older generations are supported by the legacy kms modules (radeonkms /
i915kms) in base or by installing graphics/drm-legacy-kmod.


# cat /etc/group |grep video
video:*:44:cd

# startx
xauth:  file /root/.serverauth.36414 does not exist
